subject: Buffalo ISD - Menu Planning Cycle

turns.author: Sarah Corbin <corbinsj@buffaloisd.net>
turns.body_text: District called for assistance with Weekly Nutrient analysis calculations
turns.body_html: District called for assistance with Weekly Nutrient analysis calculations

turns.author: agent:22034350878
turns.body_text: walked through Breakfast Highschool Jan 2026 cycle - discussed her entree vs grain and the serve with feature for ensuring the grains went with the right meal. This lowered her exceeded count closer (10-12 instead of 15-18).  It still exceeds but she is ok with this.  then reviewed the calorie count as this had dropped lower  - Targeting Mon / Wed for below 450 counts   - explained thu both entrees around 150-170 so need higher calorie item or eliminate lower calorie fruit + to reach the actual range (also milk quantities, less 1% more chocolate).  She reached weekly targets (monday still at 426?)     She had what she needed for today and will call back if further assistance needed.
turns.body_html:
walked through Breakfast Highschool Jan 2026 cycle - discussed her entree vs grain and the serve with feature for ensuring the grains went with the right meal. This lowered her exceeded count closer (10-12 instead of 15-18).  It still exceeds but she is ok with this.
then reviewed the calorie count as this had dropped lower
- Targeting Mon / Wed for below 450 counts
 - explained thu both entrees around 150-170 so need higher calorie item or eliminate lower calorie fruit + to reach the actual range (also milk quantities, less 1% more chocolate).
She reached weekly targets (monday still at 426?)

She had what she needed for today and will call back if further assistance needed.

subject: [EXT]Alcorn School District

turns.author: Laurie Matlock <lauriematlock@alcornschools.org>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

I am working on my cycle menus for the spring semester. When I open Primero, it will not allow me to create a new cycle menu. So I copied the middle/high school menu and changed the name, but it won't let me choose a menu within the menu cycle. The "choose menu" options are grey where they are normally purple. I also noticed that all of the high school menus that I created for spring have :1603 at the end of each menu name. I did not add the colon or numbers to the title.

I have updated my chrome and rebooted my computer, but I'm still having the same issue. I have attached screenshots. Please advise.

[photo]

Laurie Matlock
Child Nutrition Director,
Alcorn School District

[icon] 662.286.8566<tel:662.286.8566>

[icon] alcornschools.org<https://alcornschools.org/>

[icon] lauriematlock@alcornschools.org<mailto:lauriematlock@alcornschools.org>

[icon] 31 CR 401 Corinth, MS 38834<https://maps.google.com/?q=31%20CR%20401%20Corinth,%20MS%2038834>

turns.body_html: District called with questions related to the Nutrient Analysis pulled from Menu Cycle from audit review

turns.author: agent:22034350878
turns.body_text: Example - her analysis reports from under cycle were showing 0 plan counts and auditor is wanting to see counts. Explained difference in MP plan counts vs Production Plan counts. That pre-production analysis will pull the counts from Production but under cycle it looks at the menu planning menu entries.
turns.body_html: Example - her analysis reports from under cycle were showing 0 plan counts and auditor is wanting to see counts. Explained difference in MP plan counts vs Production Plan counts. That pre-production analysis will pull the counts from Production but under cycle it looks at the menu planning menu entries.

turns.body_text:
Hi Katina,     Please check your role permissions in System > Sites and Users > Roles.   You should have the option to Save any changes, such as making a cycle active/inactive            If you don't see this option, please check your permissions in System > Sites and Users > Roles under Menu Planning.   If you need any assistance, please give us a call at 866.442.6030.      ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation

Please check your role permissions in System > Sites and Users > Roles.   You should have the option to Save any changes, such as making a cycle active/inactive

subject: GREENBRIER COUNTY SCHOOLS - menu cycles

turns.author: Heather Lively <hdlively@k12.wv.us>
turns.body_text: Heather Lively                    I have created menus on Primero and now I am changing my week sequence to 4 weeks instead of 7.  Is there a way to delete menu cycles I do not use anymore.  I know I can discontinue a menu but I need the whole cycle gone.           07:17 AM, 19th Dec                   Keith                   In PrimeroEdge, menu cycles (and individual menus) are designed with data integrity and historical reporting in mind; therefore, they cannot be completely deleted or removed from the system.  However, you can effectively "remove" them from your active workspace by discontinuing them. Once a menu cycle is discontinued, it will no longer appear in your active lists, calendars, or search results unless you specifically choose to view inactive items.  How to Discontinue a Menu Cycle Since you are transitioning from a 7-week to a 4-week sequence, follow these steps for each of the old 7-week cycles you no longer need:  Navigate to Menu Cycles: Go to Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles.  Find the Cycle: Search for the 7-week menu cycle you wish to remove.  Open and Deactivate:  Click on the Name of the menu cycle to open its details.  Locate the Is Active checkbox.  Uncheck the "Is Active" box to make it inactive.  Save Changes: Click the Save button in the upper right corner.  Managing Your View To ensure your active list remains clean and only shows your new 4-week cycles:  On the Menu Cycles search page, ensure the option Include Discontinued Menu Cycles is cleared (unchecked). This will prevent the old cycles from appearing in your results.  If you ever need to reference an old cycle for historical data or to copy its contents, you can check that box to make them visible again.  Pro-Tip for Changing Week Sequences: If you find that you cannot edit the number of weeks in an existing cycle after it has been saved, the standard procedure is to discontinue the old cycle and recreate a new one with the correct number of weeks (4 weeks in your case). This ensures your nutritional analysis and calendar assignments remain accurate for the new duration.           07:25 AM, 19th Dec                     Heather Lively                    thanks           07:27 AM, 19th Dec                   Keith                   Let me know how it goes Heather           07:28 AM, 19th Dec                                          Take to Team Inbox
turns.body_html:
Heather Lively
I have created menus on Primero and now I am changing my week sequence to 4 weeks instead of 7. Is there a way to delete menu cycles I do not use anymore. I know I can discontinue a menu but I need the whole cycle gone.
07:17 AM, 19th Dec
Keith
In PrimeroEdge, menu cycles (and individual menus) are designed with data integrity and historical reporting in mind; therefore, they cannot be completely deleted or removed from the system.

However, you can effectively "remove" them from your active workspace by discontinuing them. Once a menu cycle is discontinued, it will no longer appear in your active lists, calendars, or search results unless you specifically choose to view inactive items.

How to Discontinue a Menu Cycle
Since you are transitioning from a 7-week to a 4-week sequence, follow these steps for each of the old 7-week cycles you no longer need:

Navigate to Menu Cycles: Go to Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles.

Find the Cycle: Search for the 7-week menu cycle you wish to remove.

Open and Deactivate:

Click on the Name of the menu cycle to open its details.

Locate the Is Active checkbox.

Uncheck the "Is Active" box to make it inactive.

Save Changes: Click the Save button in the upper right corner.

Managing Your View
To ensure your active list remains clean and only shows your new 4-week cycles:

On the Menu Cycles search page, ensure the option Include Discontinued Menu Cycles is cleared (unchecked). This will prevent the old cycles from appearing in your results.

If you ever need to reference an old cycle for historical data or to copy its contents, you can check that box to make them visible again.

Pro-Tip for Changing Week Sequences: If you find that you cannot edit the number of weeks in an existing cycle after it has been saved, the standard procedure is to discontinue the old cycle and recreate a new one with the correct number of weeks (4 weeks in your case). This ensures your nutritional analysis and calendar assignments remain accurate for the new duration.
07:25 AM, 19th Dec
Heather Lively
thanks
07:27 AM, 19th Dec
Keith
Let me know how it goes Heather
07:28 AM, 19th Dec

Hello Heather,

Sorry, the email sent did not include a screen shot of the chat from last week, but it was a follow up to make sure you were able to get the menu cycles adjusted as needed. Just for reference, below are the details Keith had sent:

In PrimeroEdge, menu cycles (and individual menus) are designed with data integrity and historical reporting in mind; therefore, they cannot be completely deleted or removed from the system. However, you can effectively "remove" them from your active workspace by discontinuing them. Once a menu cycle is discontinued, it will no longer appear in your active lists, calendars, or search results unless you specifically choose to view inactive items.

How to Discontinue a Menu Cycle
Since you are transitioning from a 7-week to a 4-week sequence, follow these steps for each of the old 7-week cycles you no longer need:

Navigate to Menu Cycles: Go to Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles.
Find the Cycle: Search for the 7-week menu cycle you wish to remove.
Open and Deactivate: Click on the Name of the menu cycle to open its details.
Locate the Is Active checkbox.
Uncheck the "Is Active" box to make it inactive.
Save Changes: Click the Save button in the upper right corner.

Managing Your View
To ensure your active list remains clean and only shows your new 4-week cycles:
On the Menu Cycles search page, ensure the option Include Discontinued Menu Cycles is cleared (unchecked). This will prevent the old cycles from appearing in your results.
If you ever need to reference an old cycle for historical data or to copy its contents, you can check that box to make them visible again.

Pro-Tip for Changing Week Sequences: If you find that you cannot edit the number of weeks in an existing cycle after it has been saved, the standard procedure is to discontinue the old cycle and recreate a new one with the correct number of weeks (4 weeks in your case). This ensures your nutritional analysis and calendar assignments remain accurate for the new duration.

subject: Del Mar Union School District - Request for Cycle Menu How-To Guide for SchoolCafe Publishing

turns.author: Jamie Phillips <jphillips@dmusd.org>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
 
 
 
 
Good morning, 
 
I’m reaching out to see if you have a how-to guide or documentation on creating a cycle menu using existing menu items in PrimeroEdge, as well as the steps needed to prepare and publish that menu to SchoolCafe. This information would be used to support a new
 nutrition services supervisor who will be starting soon, and having a clear guide or reference would be extremely helpful for training and onboarding. I'm sure documentation already exists, but I'm having difficulty finding a quick guide in the resources section.
 Thank you for your help. 
 
 
 
 
Have a wonderful day, 
 
 
 
 
 
Jamie Phillips, CSRM, SNS
 
 
Director of Nutrition Services & Risk Management 
 
(858) 523-6031

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Jamie,     One item I have attached, this can be found under Resources>type schoolcafe> click SchoolCafe Library> SchoolCafe admin resources> Next> then "Make your Menus look great". This would be about where to update information in the Menu item which would appear on SchoolCafe.     After Menu Items are set, for your question on cycles and publishing I might suggest Resources>search Menu and click Menu Planning and Production Library > then Menu Planning Resources > Either Training Manual or User Manual should have information on the areas you are seeking. They do reference screenshots of the prior look of Primeroedge but general functionality should be the same.    This manual has a quick over view of Menu planning cycle then also details on the areas in question from Section 3 (pg 81-119)       I will check if there may be some other resources to share which would cover from Primeroedge to SchoolCafe perhaps with the new look     Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here         Thank you,  CarrieLee Sparks | CYBERSOFT  Customer Care Specialist, Customer and Expert Care  Support: 866.442.6030 | Email: customercare@primeroedge.com   PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé  Check out our refresher
training courses in the Primeroedge Academy

subject: Olathe Public Schools- Assigning menus

turns.author: Susan Reihs <sreihsfpc@olatheschools.org>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
 
 
 
 
Hello, 
 
We have a question regarding the assigned menus when there is an unexpected no school day due to bad weather.  In our case, Monday was no school and so we are pushing that menu to today and Tuesdays to Wednesday, Wednesday to Thursday and removing the original
 menu assigned to Thursday; Friday will be back on schedule. We are wondering what options we have in how to manage these changes. Is there something other than re-assigning menus and re-entering the production planning for each item? 
 
Thank you,  
 
  
 
 
Susan Reihs 
 Olathe Public Schools Food Services 
 5005-6010 
 913-780-7005 
 sreihsfpc@olatheschools.org 
 
CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This message is from the Olathe Public Schools. The message and any attachments may be confidential or privileged and are intended only for the individual or entity identified above as the addressee. If you are not the addressee, or
 if this message has been addressed to you in error, you are not authorized to read, copy or distribute this message or any attachments. We ask that you please delete this message and any attachments and notify the sender by return email or by phone (913) 780-7000.

Yolissa
Honestly, speaking from my personal testing and experience, put them all into a menu cycle, and then deactivate the menu cycle and when you go back into that menu cycle there is a button that says deactivate all menus within the menu cycle and it does multiple at a time instead of you having to do them all yourself one by one.

They are all in a cycle now. Maybe I am missing a step?

if you deactivate the cycle, you then have to go back into the deactivated cycle to see it, which cycle is it?

We have a bunch I start with cycles and removed all that were old. I still have menus hanging out there.

yes, so look at your discontinued cycles to see them again

HS BFAST 22-23 is one I reactivated and deactivated it already

Yes, did that

then when clicking into the discontinued cycle from the purple title link, you see this discontinue mensu button here:
04:15 PM, 28th Jan

that it, thank you

No problem, glad I could help!

subject: CORRY AREA SCHOOL DISTRICT-Question with assigning menus

turns.author: Amanda Ongley <aongley@thenutritiongroup.biz>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

I am running into an issue with assigning menus.  It has been ongoing since (at least) last year and I have just worked around it, but I would like help to do it correctly. Here are the steps I follow:

Sign in to menu planning
Select assign menus (my menus are assigned on a 6 week rotation for the entire school year)
Arrow ahead to the next month
Select the menu I want to edit from the top
Select the day
Click on the blue link "Refined TNG..."
Click on Archive and Edit
The box that comes up is the Primero Edge Edit Menu box
Scroll down and unselect dates that have not yet happened or production records have not been completed
(Usually we are 1-3 days behind with entering production records in Primero)
If those boxes are unslected and I click OK, I feel like the change I am about to make should not affect the dates that are unselected.

Right now, if I make the change, even if I unslect the dates that haven't happened yet or production records haven't been completed yet, it will change the menu for that day, which causes multiple problems.

Looking at this with Lynn today, using the March menus as an example did not work because February is a short month.  If you scroll ahead to April 1st, and follow the steps I went through, you should be able to unslect the box for 3/25/26 and 2/18/26.  Once unslected, I feel that the menu for February and March should not change when I change my menu item on April 1st.

I know this is a little confusing, but hopefully I explained it okay.  I am also more than willing to walk through the steps with you over the phone, if that is better.  I am working on end of month, so I should be at my desk most of the day today!  Thank you for your help looking into this for me!



Amanda Ongley
The Nutrition Group

DFN Corry Area School District

814-664-4677 ext 3122
 814-873-7483

[Logo]

turns.body_text: I've tested this in training - and it's working the way it's supposed to.   Selecting February 27 as the ONLY date in the list that I want to impact - that's the day that gets changed when I edit the new version of the menu.   Viewing the other dates (2/20 & 3/6 - they are unchanged)     I've attached a video showing the process for using Archive & Edit - and showing that the date selected is the one changed.   I have NOT performed this in their production environment.   The training environment where it is definitely working correctly is 15.17.2  The current production environment where she is working is 15.16.2     These are the URL's used by this district - they do not perform menu planning/production in PA environment.   https://nutritiongroup.primeroedge.com/  https://nutritiongroupuat.primeroedge.com/

On Tue, Feb 3 at 9:11 AM , Amanda Ongley <aongley@thenutritiongroup.biz> wrote:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
I am running into an issue with assigning menus.  It has been ongoing since (at least) last year and I have just worked around it, but I would like help to do it correctly. Here are the steps I follow:

Sign in to menu planning
Select assign menus (my menus are assigned on a 6 week rotation for the entire school year)
Arrow ahead to the next month
Select the menu I want to edit from the top
Select the day
Click on the blue link "Refined TNG..."
Click on Archive and Edit
The box that comes up is the Primero Edge Edit Menu box
Scroll down and unselect dates that have not yet happened or production records have not been completed
(Usually we are 1-3 days behind with entering production records in Primero)
If those boxes are unslected and I click OK, I feel like the change I am about to make should not affect the dates that are unselected.

Right now, if I make the change, even if I unslect the dates that haven't happened yet or production records haven't been completed yet, it will change the menu for that day, which causes multiple problems.  

Looking at this with Lynn today, using the March menus as an example did not work because February is a short month.  If you scroll ahead to April 1st, and follow the steps I went through, you should be able to unslect the box for 3/25/26 and 2/18/26.  Once unslected, I feel that the menu for February and March should not change when I change my menu item on April 1st.  

I know this is a little confusing, but hopefully I explained it okay.  I am also more than willing to walk through the steps with you over the phone, if that is better.  I am working on end of month, so I should be at my desk most of the day today!  Thank you for your help looking into this for me!  


Amanda Ongley
The Nutrition Group
DFN Corry Area School District
814-664-4677 ext 3122
 814-873-7483

On Tue, Feb 3 at 9:11 AM , Amanda Ongley <aongley@thenutritiongroup.biz> wrote:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
I am running into an issue with assigning menus.  It has been ongoing since (at least) last year and I have just worked around it, but I would like help to do it correctly. Here are the steps I follow:
 
Sign in to menu planning
Select assign menus (my menus are assigned on a 6 week rotation for the entire school year)
Arrow ahead to the next month
Select the menu I want to edit from the top
Select the day
Click on the blue link "Refined TNG..."
Click on Archive and Edit
The box that comes up is the Primero Edge Edit Menu box
Scroll down and unselect dates that have not yet happened or production records have not been completed
(Usually we are 1-3 days behind with entering production records in Primero)
If those boxes are unslected and I click OK, I feel like the change I am about to make should not affect the dates that are unselected.
 
Right now, if I make the change, even if I unslect the dates that haven't happened yet or production records haven't been completed yet, it will change the menu for that day, which causes multiple problems.  
 
Looking at this with Lynn today, using the March menus as an example did not work because February is a short month.  If you scroll ahead to April 1st, and follow the steps I went through, you should be able to unslect the box for 3/25/26 and 2/18/26.  Once unslected, I feel that the menu for February and March should not change when I change my menu item on April 1st.  
 
I know this is a little confusing, but hopefully I explained it okay.  I am also more than willing to walk through the steps with you over the phone, if that is better.  I am working on end of month, so I should be at my desk most of the day today!  Thank you for your help looking into this for me!  
 
 
Amanda Ongley
The Nutrition Group
DFN Corry Area School District
814-664-4677 ext 3122
 814-873-7483
 
310869:507611

subject: HOLMES COUNTY CONSOLIDATED SCHOOL DISTRICT - MS-  Recipe nutrients are off for certain days of a Menu Cycle

turns.author: Charity Williams <cwilliams@holmesccsd.org>
turns.body_text: The affected Menu Cycle was k-8 Light Week, days 2 and 3; found that the Number of Servings in General tab of the Entrees was incorrect, advised whoever maintains these Recipes needs to change from 100 to 1 and update to fix the reports.

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: Reference tickets: https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/235885  https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/217649     During testing on UAT, I encountered an issue when attempting to create a copy of a menu cycle containing menus with numbers and colons preceding their names. The system displays an error message: "The Menu naming convention you are using does not allow this Menu Cycle to be copied. Please rename 1 or more Menus and try again." However, I was able to successfully copy menu cycles without numbers or colons at the beginning of menu names.  From the ticket 235885, getting the reply: (Got permission from Alekya to send the reply).           Our Developers have examined the issue and suggest:   ALL menus with a number and colon in the name should be renamed.  This is especially crucial for menus with numbers and colons at the beginning of their names.  The system automatically strips leading numbers and colons, attempting to rename menus, which can result in duplicate names. When copying a Menu Cycle, the Menu naming algorithm removes any leading text (e.g., {12345:}) before attempting to copy.    To resolve this issue, please rename the menus by removing the colons and numbers. After making these changes, attempt to copy the menu cycle again and let us know if it succeeds.
turns.body_html:

https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/217649

During testing on UAT, I encountered an issue when attempting to create a copy of a menu cycle containing menus with numbers and colons preceding their names. The system displays an error message: "The Menu naming convention you are using does not allow this Menu Cycle to be copied. Please rename 1 or more Menus and try again." However, I was able to successfully copy menu cycles without numbers or colons at the beginning of menu names.
From the ticket 235885, getting the reply: (Got permission from Alekya to send the reply).



Our Developers have examined the issue and suggest:
  • ALL menus with a number and colon in the name should be renamed.
  • This is especially crucial for menus with numbers and colons at the beginning of their names.
  • The system automatically strips leading numbers and colons, attempting to rename menus, which can result in duplicate names. When copying a Menu Cycle, the Menu naming algorithm removes any leading text (e.g., {12345:}) before attempting to copy.
To resolve this issue, please rename the menus by removing the colons and numbers. After making these changes, attempt to copy the menu cycle again and let us know if it succeeds.

turns.author: agent:22004298836
turns.body_text: This is a USER error - not a software issue.  When OFFER WITH is used, the PRIMARY should be the entree.   This was set as complementary... fixing that amended the analysis.     I only did this menu, so if there are others, please tend to them.  Lunch Week 3 Day 2 :1918 - archived on Mar  5 2026 11:23AM     The screenshots in the ticket showed that the entree was marked as C (complementary)       Once I made the entree P (Primary) the analysis is correct as expected.  This was not a software issue.
turns.body_html:
This is a USER error - not a software issue.
When OFFER WITH is used, the PRIMARY should be the entree. 
This was set as complementary... fixing that amended the analysis.

I only did this menu, so if there are others, please tend to them.
Lunch Week 3 Day 2 :1918 - archived on Mar  5 2026 11:23AM

The screenshots in the ticket showed that the entree was marked as C (complementary)

Once I made the entree P (Primary) the analysis is correct as expected.
This was not a software issue.

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: The M/MA analysis was failing because the entrée was marked as complimentary. When using the OFFER WITH feature, the entrée should be marked as primary to include in the M/MA values for the analysis.           Once this change is implemented, the issue will be resolved, and the analysis will display the correct values.                When using OFFER WITH, please ensure that the entree is marked as primary for other menus as well. Kindly verify this information and inform us if you have any doubts or require further assistance.

When using OFFER WITH, please ensure that the entree is marked as primary for other menus as well. Kindly verify this information and inform us if you have any doubts or require further assistance.

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: To update a menu item from complimentary to primary or vice versa, click on the x mark shown in the screenshot below. You will be prompted with the message: "Are you sure you wish to break the Offer With link?" Click on OK.  Now, the "Offer With" button will be available beside the entrée name (e.g., BBQ Rib Sandwich). Click on the "Offer With" button, select the complimentary item (e.g., Fries, Crinkle cut), and click on Add.  Once you click on Add, the entrée will become a primary menu item and the vegetable will be complimentary. Click on save.  After this change is made, the menu analysis should display the counts correctly.           Please verify this and let us know if it's working. If you require any further assistance, please don't hesitate to ask.
turns.body_html:
1. To update a menu item from complimentary to primary or vice versa, click on the x mark shown in the screenshot below. You will be prompted with the message: "Are you sure you wish to break the Offer With link?" Click on OK.
  2. Now, the "Offer With" button will be available beside the entrée name (e.g., BBQ Rib Sandwich). Click on the "Offer With" button, select the complimentary item (e.g., Fries, Crinkle cut), and click on Add.
  3. Once you click on Add, the entrée will become a primary menu item and the vegetable will be complimentary. Click on save.
  4. After this change is made, the menu analysis should display the counts correctly.


Please verify this and let us know if it's working. If you require any further assistance, please don't hesitate to ask.

turns.body_html:
Discussed with Alekya

CASE 1: Primary and complimentary item are in same Category

(Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918)       &       (Menu Cycle : MHS Lunch February - Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918))

1) Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918 

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menus >  Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918 > Analysis  

As per the current system functionality, the menu analysis is accurate. When the primary and secondary menu items are assigned to a menu, if a student selects only the complimentary menu item without the primary menu item, the menu analysis will exclude the meal contribution of that menu item.

To illustrate with the following examples:

1. If a student selects BBQ Hawaiian Chicken, the fried rice is complimentary. The meal contribution will be (2 Meat/MA) + (1 Meat/MA + 1 Non-WGR + 1/8 Veg-O). This results in a total of 3 Meat/MA for this case.

2. If a student selects Chicken salad with merchants, the Crunchy crackers are the complimentary item. The meal contribution becomes (2 3/4 Meat/MA 1/2 Fruit 1/2 Veg-DG 1/4 Veg-RO 5/8 Veg-O) + 1 WGR. This yields 2 3/4 Meat/MA for this case.

3. If a student selects Fish Melt, the crinkle cut fries are the complimentary item. The meal contribution will be (1 Meat/MA 1 Non-WGR 1/8 Veg-O) + (1/2 Veg-S). This results in 1 Meat/MA for this case.

4. If a student selects only fried rice, the meal contribution will not be included in the analysis as it is only supposed to be complimentary to BBQ Hawaiian Chicken. It is not intended to be given on its own. Therefore, the Meat/MA analysis will be 0 for this case.

Considering all these cases, the Meat/MA analysis will give us a range of 0 - 3.000, which aligns with the value in the meal analysis.



2) Menu Cycle :  MHS Lunch February : Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918)

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918) > Analysis  



CASE 2: Entrée as a Complimentary Menu Item

Menu Cycle: MHS Lunch February - Week 2 Day 4 (Menu: Day 4 MHS Lunch), Week 3 Day 2 (Menu: Lunch Week 3 Day 2 :1918), Week 3 Day 5 (Menu: Lunch Week 3 Day 5 :1918)

The M/MA analysis was failing due to the entrée being marked as complimentary. When using the OFFER WITH feature, the entrée should be marked as primary to be included in the M/MA values for analysis. If the entrée is marked as complimentary, the menu analysis will exclude the meal contribution of that entrée, causing the M/MA analysis to fail.

2) Week 3 Day 2:

​We have verified the menu analysis for Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 (1918) and Menu Cycle: MHS Lunch February. The calculations in the menu analysis are correct, and we have provided an explanation for the calculations below. Please review the notes and let us know if you have any doubts. 

CASE 1: Primary and complimentary item are in same Category

1) Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918 

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menus >  Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918 > Analysis  

As per the current system functionality, the menu analysis is accurate. When the primary and secondary menu items are assigned to a menu, if a student selects only the complimentary menu item without the primary menu item, the menu analysis will exclude the meal contribution of that menu item.

To illustrate with the following examples:

1. If a student selects BBQ Hawaiian Chicken, the fried rice is complimentary. The meal contribution will be (2 Meat/MA) + (1 Meat/MA + 1 Non-WGR + 1/8 Veg-O). This results in a total of 3 Meat/MA for this case.

2. If a student selects Chicken salad with merchants, the Crunchy crackers are the complimentary item. The meal contribution becomes (2 3/4 Meat/MA 1/2 Fruit 1/2 Veg-DG 1/4 Veg-RO 5/8 Veg-O) + 1 WGR. This yields 2 3/4 Meat/MA for this case.

3. If a student selects Fish Melt, the crinkle cut fries are the complimentary item. The meal contribution will be (1 Meat/MA 1 Non-WGR 1/8 Veg-O) + (1/2 Veg-S). This results in 1 Meat/MA for this case.

4. If a student selects only fried rice, the meal contribution will not be included in the analysis as it is only supposed to be complimentary to BBQ Hawaiian Chicken. It is not intended to be given on its own. Therefore, the Meat/MA analysis will be 0 for this case.

Considering all these cases, the Meat/MA analysis will give us a range of 0 - 3.000, which aligns with the value in the meal analysis.



2) Menu Cycle :  MHS Lunch February : Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918)

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918) > Analysis  



CASE 2: Entrée as a Complimentary Menu Item

Menu Cycle: MHS Lunch February - Week 2 Day 4 (Menu: Day 4 MHS Lunch), Week 3 Day 2 (Menu: Lunch Week 3 Day 2 :1918), Week 3 Day 5 (Menu: Lunch Week 3 Day 5 :1918)

The M/MA analysis was failing due to the entrée being marked as complimentary. When using the OFFER WITH feature, the entrée should be marked as primary to be included in the M/MA values for analysis. If the entrée is marked as complimentary, the menu analysis will exclude the meal contribution of that entrée, causing the M/MA analysis to fail.

​

turns.body_html:
The customer wants to know why the day 3, day 4 and day 5 analysis is failing

The menu analysis for days 3, 4, and 5 is failing due to insufficient daily standard values for Meat/MA and grains. Please review the attached screenshots and explanations detailing why each menu is failing the analysis. Kindly verify this information and inform us if you require any further assistance. 


Day 3 fails to meet the daily requirement for grains. The grains value must be greater than or equal to 2.000. 
If the student chooses Tuna Salad with Crackers, that meal only provides a grains value of 1.000.



Day 4 is failing to meet the daily requirement for Meat/MA.
If a student selects only fried rice, the meal contribution will not be included in the analysis as it is intended to be complimentary to BBQ Hawaiian Chicken. The fried rice is not meant to be served on its own. Consequently, in this case, the Meat/MA analysis will result in a value of 0.



Day 5 fails to meet the daily requirement for grains. The grains value must be ≥2.000. 
If the student chooses Chicken salad with Saltine crackers, the meal only has a grain value of 1.000.

On Thu, Mar 19, 2026 at 4:51 PM customercare@primeroedge.com <customercare@primeroedge.com> wrote:
Hello ,

​We have verified the menu analysis for Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 (1918) and Menu Cycle: MHS Lunch February. The calculations in the menu analysis are correct, and we have provided an explanation for the calculations below. Please review the notes and let us know if you have any doubts. 

CASE 1: Primary and complimentary item are in same Category

1) Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918 

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menus >  Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918 > Analysis  

As per the current system functionality, the menu analysis is accurate. When the primary and secondary menu items are assigned to a menu, if a student selects only the complimentary menu item without the primary menu item, the menu analysis will exclude the meal contribution of that menu item.

To illustrate with the following examples:

1. If a student selects BBQ Hawaiian Chicken, the fried rice is complimentary. The meal contribution will be (2 Meat/MA) + (1 Meat/MA + 1 Non-WGR + 1/8 Veg-O). This results in a total of 3 Meat/MA for this case.

2. If a student selects Chicken salad with merchants, the Crunchy crackers are the complimentary item. The meal contribution becomes (2 3/4 Meat/MA 1/2 Fruit 1/2 Veg-DG 1/4 Veg-RO 5/8 Veg-O) + 1 WGR. This yields 2 3/4 Meat/MA for this case.

3. If a student selects Fish Melt, the crinkle cut fries are the complimentary item. The meal contribution will be (1 Meat/MA 1 Non-WGR 1/8 Veg-O) + (1/2 Veg-S). This results in 1 Meat/MA for this case.

4. If a student selects only fried rice, the meal contribution will not be included in the analysis as it is only supposed to be complimentary to BBQ Hawaiian Chicken. It is not intended to be given on its own. Therefore, the Meat/MA analysis will be 0 for this case.

Considering all these cases, the Meat/MA analysis will give us a range of 0 - 3.000, which aligns with the value in the meal analysis.



2) Menu Cycle :  MHS Lunch February : Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918)

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918) > Analysis  



CASE 2: Entrée as a Complimentary Menu Item

Menu Cycle: MHS Lunch February - Week 2 Day 4 (Menu: Day 4 MHS Lunch), Week 3 Day 2 (Menu: Lunch Week 3 Day 2 :1918), Week 3 Day 5 (Menu: Lunch Week 3 Day 5 :1918)

The M/MA analysis was failing due to the entrée being marked as complimentary. When using the OFFER WITH feature, the entrée should be marked as primary to be included in the M/MA values for analysis. If the entrée is marked as complimentary, the menu analysis will exclude the meal contribution of that entrée, causing the M/MA analysis to fail. 

1) Week 2 Day 4:
​
Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 2 Day 4 > Analysis  
​



2) Week 3 Day 2:
Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 3 Day 2 > Analysis  



3) Week 3 Day 5:
Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 3 Day 5 > Analysis  



Please review the notes and let us know if you have any doubts.

On Thu, Mar 19, 2026 at 4:51 PM customercare@primeroedge.com <customercare@primeroedge.com> wrote:
Hello ,

​We have verified the menu analysis for Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 (1918) and Menu Cycle: MHS Lunch February. The calculations in the menu analysis are correct, and we have provided an explanation for the calculations below. Please review the notes and let us know if you have any doubts.

CASE 1: Primary and complimentary item are in same Category

1) Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918 

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menus >  Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918 > Analysis  

As per the current system functionality, the menu analysis is accurate. When the primary and secondary menu items are assigned to a menu, if a student selects only the complimentary menu item without the primary menu item, the menu analysis will exclude the meal contribution of that menu item.

To illustrate with the following examples:

1. If a student selects BBQ Hawaiian Chicken, the fried rice is complimentary. The meal contribution will be (2 Meat/MA) + (1 Meat/MA + 1 Non-WGR + 1/8 Veg-O). This results in a total of 3 Meat/MA for this case.

2. If a student selects Chicken salad with merchants, the Crunchy crackers are the complimentary item. The meal contribution becomes (2 3/4 Meat/MA 1/2 Fruit 1/2 Veg-DG 1/4 Veg-RO 5/8 Veg-O) + 1 WGR. This yields 2 3/4 Meat/MA for this case.

3. If a student selects Fish Melt, the crinkle cut fries are the complimentary item. The meal contribution will be (1 Meat/MA 1 Non-WGR 1/8 Veg-O) + (1/2 Veg-S). This results in 1 Meat/MA for this case.

4. If a student selects only fried rice, the meal contribution will not be included in the analysis as it is only supposed to be complimentary to BBQ Hawaiian Chicken. It is not intended to be given on its own. Therefore, the Meat/MA analysis will be 0 for this case.

Considering all these cases, the Meat/MA analysis will give us a range of 0 - 3.000, which aligns with the value in the meal analysis.



2) Menu Cycle :  MHS Lunch February : Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918)

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 4 Day 4 (Menu: Lunch Week 4 Day 4 :1918) > Analysis  



CASE 2: Entrée as a Complimentary Menu Item

Menu Cycle: MHS Lunch February - Week 2 Day 4 (Menu: Day 4 MHS Lunch), Week 3 Day 2 (Menu: Lunch Week 3 Day 2 :1918), Week 3 Day 5 (Menu: Lunch Week 3 Day 5 :1918)

The M/MA analysis was failing due to the entrée being marked as complimentary. When using the OFFER WITH feature, the entrée should be marked as primary to be included in the M/MA values for analysis. If the entrée is marked as complimentary, the menu analysis will exclude the meal contribution of that entrée, causing the M/MA analysis to fail. 

1) Week 2 Day 4:

Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 2 Day 4 > Analysis  




2) Week 3 Day 2:
Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 3 Day 2 > Analysis  



3) Week 3 Day 5:
Primero MS (Version 15.16.1) > MERIDIAN PUBLIC SCHOOLS - MS > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles > MHS Lunch February > Week 3 Day 5 > Analysis  



Please review the notes and let us know if you have any doubts.

Hello ,

Apologies for the delay in response. ​Please follow these steps to update a menu item from complimentary to primary or vice versa:


  2. To update a menu item from complimentary to primary or vice versa, click on the x mark shown in the screenshot below. You will be prompted with the message: "Are you sure you wish to break the Offer With link?" Click on OK.

  4. Now, the "Offer With" button will be available beside the entrée name (e.g., BBQ Rib Sandwich). Click on the "Offer With" button, select the complimentary item (e.g., Fries, Crinkle cut), and click on Add.

  6. Once you click on Add, the entrée will become a primary menu item and the vegetable will be complimentary. Click on save.

  8. After this change is made, the menu analysis should display the counts correctly.



Please verify this and let us know if it's working. If you require any further assistance, please don't hesitate to ask.

subject: Stillwater Public Schools - Question Regarding Menu Cycles

turns.author: Brenda Elliott <belliott@stillwaterschools.com>
turns.body_text: District official reached out with a question regarding setting up menu cycles and menu lines as they're needing to consolidate a few grades into one line for the next school year.
turns.body_html: District official reached out with a question regarding setting up menu cycles and menu lines as they're needing to consolidate a few grades into one line for the next school year.

turns.author: agent:22034350878
turns.body_text: Reached out to customer per glip with Jordan where district requested a callback for further assistance.    Brenda was having some confusion around menu lines vs site configuration vs site group (thinking she had to make all new menu lines for a change in grade groups). Clarified one menu line could be used as it gets tagged in site configuration but she will have to create or copy new menus to change the grade groups on the Menu's directly that are added to the cycles since the past ones had already been served.     Discussed potentially having one grade group for jr/ms (either new or adding school to another) but as this is a single year occurrence and the grades will change again after 26-27 and where she wants to avoid any confusion for sites looking up past records, she is opting to leave them split.       Since it will be split to different site groups discussed she will need to copy the cycle to the other group b/c on assign menu searches for cycle/menu it filters by the group's choices only.   Also discussed archive edit vs copy and adding item from production side (by attached to send)
turns.body_html:
Reached out to customer per glip with Jordan where district requested a callback for further assistance.

Brenda was having some confusion around menu lines vs site configuration vs site group (thinking she had to make all new menu lines for a change in grade groups). Clarified one menu line could be used as it gets tagged in site configuration but she will have to create or copy new menus to change the grade groups on the Menu's directly that are added to the cycles since the past ones had already been served.

Discussed potentially having one grade group for jr/ms (either new or adding school to another) but as this is a single year occurrence and the grades will change again after 26-27 and where she wants to avoid any confusion for sites looking up past records, she is opting to leave them split.  

Since it will be split to different site groups discussed she will need to copy the cycle to the other group b/c on assign menu searches for cycle/menu it filters by the group's choices only. 
Also discussed archive edit vs copy and adding item from production side (by attached to send)

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Brenda,     Pleasure speaking with you and glad we were able to clarify a few questions for you. To quickly recap, at this time you are opting to leave the site groups split to Jr High and Middle school. With this you will need to copy the cycle or menus as when assigning it will filter by site group (and menu cycles are specific by this as well). However, you should not need to create a new menu line and the menu lines used are by what is added under site configuration.       Here is a reference for a few differences on Archive and Edit vs Copying a menu in case it should assist that we discussed.  I will close this ticket, but should you have any further questions or issues regarding this, let us know by replying all to this ticket or calling 1.866.442.6030 M-F 6a-6p and reference this ticket 315380.     Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here        Thank you,  CarrieLee Sparks | CYBERSOFT  Customer Care Specialist, Customer and Expert Care  Support: 866.442.6030 | Email: customercare@primeroedge.com   PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé  Check out our refresher
training courses in the Primeroedge Academy
turns.body_html:
Hello Brenda,

Pleasure speaking with you and glad we were able to clarify a few questions for you. To quickly recap, at this time you are opting to leave the site groups split to Jr High and Middle school. With this you will need to copy the cycle or menus as when assigning it will filter by site group (and menu cycles are specific by this as well). However, you should not need to create a new menu line and the menu lines used are by what is added under site configuration.  

Here is a reference for a few differences on Archive and Edit vs Copying a menu in case it should assist that we discussed.  I will close this ticket, but should you have any further questions or issues regarding this, let us know by replying all to this ticket or calling 1.866.442.6030 M-F 6a-6p and reference this ticket 315380.

turns.body_text:
Please take a look at ticket #315470 raised by USA PrimeroEdge Food Systems Support (primeroedgefoodsystemssupport.usa@sodexo.com).        Hi Al,      The district rpeorted an issue with the user being able to add the Menus from a different Site group than the site group of the menu cycle itself.   This is not an isolated case, this has happened before and it was reported in the below reference tickets :     https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/265891  https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/314325  In this case, the Menu Cycle was for the site group "Taste 4", but the user was able to add the Menus from "FoodiE Cafe".     Production  Super User Login  Central Realm  Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles  Texas > 2014+ Meal Pattern > Lunch > Taste4  Menu Cycle: Texas SY25-26 Grill-CORE- T4/FoodiE MTO     Two FoodiE Cafe Site Group (grade 6-8) Menus are in the Taste4 Site Group (grades 9-12) menu cycle.             soTraining was updated with a copy from Production last week and the menus are correct.             So, We have verified the data in the background, and it seems that the menus on the Menu Cycle on SO-PROD was modified on 03/26/2026 by user "Machelle Andrew".      So-Training has the copy of the menu cycle from before the modifications because the SoTraining was done on March 16th with the copy of So-PROD as of that date when the modifications haven't happened yet.      Here is the details of what we were able to lookup in regards to this Menu Cycle in question.                 And then the user fixed the menus. But Nicole wants to know who is the user able to choose menus from a different site group when the system should not allow it in the first place.      I have tried to reproduce the issue with multiple menu cycles on SoTraining and SoMigration enviornments, but I was not able to make any successful attempts to reproduce the issue.      However, I have stumbled up on weird scenario where on of the Incorrect menus that the user was able to add to the menu cycle from a different site group (FoodiE Cafe) , was originally copied/ archived from a menu that belonged to the site group Taste4 initially. This is just a hunch. But I am still not sure why that would have provided the user the ability to choose the menu from a different site group after all.      This is the Menu Cycle that belongs to "Taste 4" site group  on which the user was able to select the menu from incorrect site group https://so.primeroedge.com/MenuPlanning/MenuCycleInfo.aspx?MenuCycleId=49708&FromMenuCycle=true   This is the menu from the Incorrect site group (FoodiE Cafe):   https://so.primeroedge.com/MenuPlanning/MenusNew.aspx?MenuId=784988&FromMenuListing=true      This is the Original menu that belonged to Site Group (Taste 4) and was archived and copied multiple times which ultimately landed on the above menu:  https://so.primeroedge.com/MenuPlanning/MenusNew.aspx?MenuId=728729      Can you please look into this to see what is causing the issues.   ​   ​   Thank you,  Alekya Kotta | CYBERSOFT  Expert Care Team

turns.body_html:
Please take a look at ticket #315470 raised by USA PrimeroEdge Food Systems Support (primeroedgefoodsystemssupport.usa@sodexo.com).

Hi Al, 

The district rpeorted an issue with the user being able to add the Menus from a different Site group than the site group of the menu cycle itself. 
This is not an isolated case, this has happened before and it was reported in the below reference tickets : 

https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/265891
https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/314325

In this case, the Menu Cycle was for the site group "Taste 4", but the user was able to add the Menus from "FoodiE Cafe".

Production
Super User Login
Central Realm
Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycles
Texas > 2014+ Meal Pattern > Lunch > Taste4
Menu Cycle: Texas SY25-26 Grill-CORE- T4/FoodiE MTO

Two FoodiE Cafe Site Group (grade 6-8) Menus are in the Taste4 Site Group (grades 9-12) menu cycle.



soTraining was updated with a copy from Production last week and the menus are correct.



So, We have verified the data in the background, and it seems that the menus on the Menu Cycle on SO-PROD was modified on 03/26/2026 by user "Machelle Andrew". 

So-Training has the copy of the menu cycle from before the modifications because the SoTraining was done on March 16th with the copy of So-PROD as of that date when the modifications haven't happened yet. 

Here is the details of what we were able to lookup in regards to this Menu Cycle in question. 




And then the user fixed the menus. But Nicole wants to know who is the user able to choose menus from a different site group when the system should not allow it in the first place. 

I have tried to reproduce the issue with multiple menu cycles on SoTraining and SoMigration enviornments, but I was not able to make any successful attempts to reproduce the issue. 

However, I have stumbled up on weird scenario where on of the Incorrect menus that the user was able to add to the menu cycle from a different site group (FoodiE Cafe) , was originally copied/ archived from a menu that belonged to the site group Taste4 initially. This is just a hunch. But I am still not sure why that would have provided the user the ability to choose the menu from a different site group after all. 

This is the Menu Cycle that belongs to "Taste 4" site group  on which the user was able to select the menu from incorrect site group https://so.primeroedge.com/MenuPlanning/MenuCycleInfo.aspx?MenuCycleId=49708&FromMenuCycle=true

This is the menu from the Incorrect site group (FoodiE Cafe): 
https://so.primeroedge.com/MenuPlanning/MenusNew.aspx?MenuId=784988&FromMenuListing=true

This is the Original menu that belonged to Site Group (Taste 4) and was archived and copied multiple times which ultimately landed on the above menu: 
https://so.primeroedge.com/MenuPlanning/MenusNew.aspx?MenuId=728729 

Can you please look into this to see what is causing the issues. 
​
​
Thank you,
Alekya Kotta | CYBERSOFT
Expert Care Team Lead, Expert Care
Support: 866.442.6030 | Email: customercare@primeroedge.com 
PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé

turns.author: Tyler Frey <tyfrey@pa.gov>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Hello,

This email is solely to provide feedback on the update from last night. Due to the update, there is now a drop down for menus in the menu planning - menu cycle section. See below.

[cid:image001.png@01DCBDCF.7E1186B0]

Previously, you could select and edit the menus by clicking the menu name. With this update, I am now required to go out and search each individual menu in the menu section to be able to edit them. It will greatly increase the effort involved in building a menu cycle as I cannot move from one menu to the next to modify them. Allowing editing of the menus in this section was very beneficial and it would be great if that function could be returned.

Thanks!


[PA DHS Keystone Icon]
Tyler Frey RD, LDN
Dietary Management Services Specialist

Department of Human Services | Bureau of Juvenile Justice Services
8 Opportunity Drive
Loysville PA 17047
Work Cell: (717) 585-4980


If you suspect child abuse or neglect, please call ChildLine to make a confidential report at: 1-800-932-0313

turns.author: Darlene Lopez <dlopezmonge@ems-isd.net>
turns.body_text: Darlene Lopez                    Hello! There seems to have been an update on meny cycles. I am working on my summer menus. Now, within the cycles, I can't make menu names changes there. It forces me to choose from current existing names. The only way I can make menu changes is by searching that menu type on a whole new tab. This is not very time efficient.           11:22 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    Like I can't select the menu itself there anymore and make the necessary modifications.           11:24 AM, 30th Mar                   Jared                   Thank you for contacting customer care. I hope you're having a great day!           11:24 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    Hi Jared! I hope you are doing well.           11:25 AM, 30th Mar                   Jared                   I think I know what you are referring to, on the page in question, do you see the serving groups listed there as a link? You should be able to click those to edit the menus now           11:25 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    That worked! Thanks.           11:27 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    I wonder why it got changed. It wasn't broken I guess           11:27 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    Also, do you know why a lot of my current menu items turned red?           11:27 AM, 30th Mar                   Jared                   It is to make it easier to swap menus on the the fly in case that is needed, and we are open to feedback           11:28 AM, 30th Mar                   Jared                   The menu items being red is to indicate that some part of the item, from the recipe to one of the ingredients to the stock items is inactive or discontinued           11:28 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    Which I can see that!           11:29 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    That's what we have known but it is weird that some of our current items turned innactive and were active as we use them all year.           11:30 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    Thank you for your help, Jared!           11:31 AM, 30th Mar                   Jared                   Of course and anytime! If you have any other questions or find any items that you may need assistance with I am here to help           11:32 AM, 30th Mar                   Jared                   Until next time           11:32 AM, 30th Mar                     Darlene Lopez                    Thank you!           11:32 AM, 30th Mar                                          Take to Team Inbox
turns.body_html:
Darlene Lopez
Hello! There seems to have been an update on meny cycles. I am working on my summer menus. Now, within the cycles, I can't make menu names changes there. It forces me to choose from current existing names. The only way I can make menu changes is by searching that menu type on a whole new tab. This is not very time efficient.
11:22 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
Like I can't select the menu itself there anymore and make the necessary modifications.
11:24 AM, 30th Mar
Jared
Thank you for contacting customer care. I hope you're having a great day!
11:24 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
Hi Jared! I hope you are doing well.
11:25 AM, 30th Mar
Jared
I think I know what you are referring to, on the page in question, do you see the serving groups listed there as a link? You should be able to click those to edit the menus now
11:25 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
That worked! Thanks.
11:27 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
I wonder why it got changed. It wasn't broken I guess
11:27 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
Also, do you know why a lot of my current menu items turned red?
11:27 AM, 30th Mar
Jared
It is to make it easier to swap menus on the the fly in case that is needed, and we are open to feedback
11:28 AM, 30th Mar
Jared
The menu items being red is to indicate that some part of the item, from the recipe to one of the ingredients to the stock items is inactive or discontinued
11:28 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
Which I can see that!
11:29 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
That's what we have known but it is weird that some of our current items turned innactive and were active as we use them all year.
11:30 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
Thank you for your help, Jared!
11:31 AM, 30th Mar
Jared
Of course and anytime! If you have any other questions or find any items that you may need assistance with I am here to help
11:32 AM, 30th Mar
Jared
Until next time
11:32 AM, 30th Mar
Darlene Lopez
Thank you!
11:32 AM, 30th Mar
Take to Team Inbox

turns.body_html:
Hello Kathleen,
So it seems that in the last version update done fairly recently updated the menu cycle page and it seems that you can click on the serving group as the clickable link that used to the menu name where you could make changes as you mentioned. I have included a screenshot of the patch notes with the indicted changes. 

If you click on the hyperlink, it will take you to the menu assigned to the day and let you modify but in this case it shows that a production record has been saved (this is to keep the previous record preserved)  and will require you to achieve and edit a new menu with the changes to the menu item you would like. You can then go back to the menu cycle and click on the drop down or choose menus for the new one you saved. Let me know if you have questions.

subject: PR: 16.4 - PrimeroEdge - SoWa County Schools - Red Menu Items, ingredients not discontinued

turns.author: Roberta Grummons <rgrummon@sowashco.org>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
 
 
 
 
 Hello, 
   
 We have a couple of issues with PrimeroEdge and I have a feeling it was maybe due to an update, but I am not 100% sure. 
 
   
 When you go into the menu cycle tab, then click on the menu cycle you wish to work on, you cannot adjust the menu from here anymore all I have is a drop down to change out the menu.  The previous version made it easier to adjust the nutrition
 analysis etc…  Is this something that could be changed back to how it was? 
   
   
   
  
   
 The other problem we are having is there are a few items that are now shown as red and discontinued.  One of these items is 1% milk and our elementary menus are fine and as they were, but the middle and high school menu cycles now have
 a discontinued milk and we have not altered these menus in any way to cause this to occur.  Then when I go in to do find and replace to the correct item, it will say it occurred, but it really didn’t. 
 
   
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
   



 
 

 
 
 
 
Bobbie Jo Grummons RD, LD, SNS
 
 
Assistant Director of Nutrition Services
 
   
 
South Washington County Schools
 
 
rgrummon@sowashco.org 
 
 
P: 651-425-6250    C: 651-336-1206   F: 651-425-6312
 
 sowashco.org 
 
7362 E Point Douglas Road South, Cottage Grove, MN 55016
 
 


 
 
COTTAGE GROVE  |  NEWPORT  |  ST. PAUL PARK  |  WOODBURY  |  AFTON  |  DENMARK  |  GREY CLOUD ISLAND
turns.body_html:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Hello,
 
We have a couple of issues with PrimeroEdge and I have a feeling it was maybe due to an update, but I am not 100% sure. 
 
When you go into the menu cycle tab, then click on the menu cycle you wish to work on, you cannot adjust the menu from here anymore all I have is a drop down to change out the menu.  The previous version made it easier to adjust the nutrition analysis etc…  Is this something that could be changed back to how it was?
 
 
 
 
The other problem we are having is there are a few items that are now shown as red and discontinued.  One of these items is 1% milk and our elementary menus are fine and as they were, but the middle and high school menu cycles now have a discontinued milk and we have not altered these menus in any way to cause this to occur.  Then when I go in to do find and replace to the correct item, it will say it occurred, but it really didn’t. 
 
 
Bobbie Jo Grummons RD, LD, SNS
Assistant Director of Nutrition Services
 
South Washington County Schools
rgrummon@sowashco.org 
P: 651-425-6250    C: 651-336-1206   F: 651-425-6312

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Roberta,     There was and Enhancement with the recent update which did cause a change to where you click to view the menu. You may find more details on in the release notes (click Resources> Type Release > click on Release 16 link).  With this, the hyperlink to see and connect directly to the attached menu was shifted to the Grade Group in purple.  If you click there it should take you to the Menu just as before.   This enhancement was to provide 2 options for how to attach or select a menu in a cycle.          We apologize for any confusion with this change.  Please let me know if you click where it says 6-8, Adult that all works for you like before or if you have any further questions.      Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket         Thank you,  CarrieLee Sparks | CYBERSOFT  Customer Care Specialist, Customer and Expert Care  Support: 866.442.6030 | Email: customercare@primeroedge.com   PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé  Check out our refresher
training courses in the Primeroedge Academy
turns.body_html:
Hello Roberta,

There was and Enhancement with the recent update which did cause a change to where you click to view the menu. You may find more details on in the release notes (click Resources> Type Release > click on Release 16 link).  With this, the hyperlink to see and connect directly to the attached menu was shifted to the Grade Group in purple.  If you click there it should take you to the Menu just as before.   This enhancement was to provide 2 options for how to attach or select a menu in a cycle. 

We apologize for any confusion with this change.  Please let me know if you click where it says 6-8, Adult that all works for you like before or if you have any further questions.

Ok, this makes sense on the cycle menu.  On a different note, did you find any reason why our milk, syrup, and other items are randomly discontinued?

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Roberta,     There is a bug identified with the Red items containing discontinued ingredients, which will be rectified in version 16.4.     The issue we found is that the code was not looking for ONLY the "current" ingredients in the menu item... it was looking at any ingredient that had EVER been associated with it. So if you (appropriately) previously replaced an "old" milk with a "new" milk on the menu item milk, it's flagging that milk. The update coming in 16.4 will look at only CURRENT ingredients in the menu items, so those instances will no longer be flagged, and only "actual" menu items that need to be looked into will be red.              Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here     Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket          Schrenia Bockholt | CYBERSOFT  Business Analyst, Product Management  PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé
turns.body_html:
Hello Roberta,

There is a bug identified with the Red items containing discontinued ingredients, which will be rectified in version 16.4.

The issue we found is that the code was not looking for ONLY the "current" ingredients in the menu item... it was looking at any ingredient that had EVER been associated with it. So if you (appropriately) previously replaced an "old" milk with a "new" milk on the menu item milk, it's flagging that milk. The update coming in 16.4 will look at only CURRENT ingredients in the menu items, so those instances will no longer be flagged, and only "actual" menu items that need to be looked into will be red.

subject: PrimeroEdge MS - RANKIN CO SCHOOL DISTRICT - MS - Request to better track non-program adults in planning/production

turns.author: Lei510 <lei510@rcsd.ms>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Dear PrimeroEdge Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I would like to submit a request within the production plan/record feature of PrimeroEdge. Currently, we are able to effectively document reimbursable meals; however, we are seeking a more streamlined way to track and document non-program adult meals directly within the production record.

Adding a designated section or field for non-program adults would:

  *   Improve accuracy in daily meal accountability
  *   Support compliance with program regulations and audit requirements
  *   Provide clearer documentation for financial reconciliation of non-reimbursable meals

At this time, we are tracking this information separately, which creates additional steps and increases the risk of inconsistencies.

We believe this enhancement would be beneficial not only for our district but for other districts managing both reimbursable and non-reimbursable meal services.

Thank you for your consideration of this request. Please let me know if any additional information or clarification is needed.


[image.png]


[https://ci3.googleusercontent.com/mail-sig/AIorK4znVRNyHUvfWeIy5nr-Lu87jHcZ4t_BvaEWQ9Pg_yqC-3EDddOci6IJNi5o4ZKBYwGTSY5E-bO7aKpz]

Rankin County School District

Mission: Bring Everyone's Strengths Together!  We will all intentionally focus on empowering our students to reach their maximum potential by embracing opportunities and challenges while cultivating a tradition of distinction in education.

Vision: Continue a tradition of excellence by providing a world-class education that empowers all to grow through curiosity, discovery, and learning.





This email and any attachments to it may be confidential and are intended solely for the use of the individual to whom it is addressed. If you are not the intended recipient of this email, you must neither take any action based upon its contents, nor copy or show it to anyone. Please contact the sender if you believe you have received this email in error.

subject: South Washington County - Accessing Menus from Menu cycle

turns.author: Roberta Grummons <rgrummon@sowashco.org>
turns.body_text:
Hello,   We have a couple of issues with PrimeroEdge and I have a feeling it was maybe due to an update, but I am not 100% sure.    When you go into the menu cycle tab, then click on the menu cycle you wish to work on, you cannot adjust the menu from here anymore all I have is a drop down to change out the menu.  The previous version made it easier to adjust the nutrition analysis etc…  Is this something that could be changed back to how it was?        The other problem we are having is there are a few items that are now shown as red and discontinued.  One of these items is 1% milk and our elementary menus are fine and as they were, but the middle and high school menu cycles now have a discontinued milk and we have not altered these menus in any way to cause this to occur.  Then when I go in to do find and replace to the correct item, it will say it occurred, but it really didn’t.     

  
 Bobbie Jo Grummons RD, LD, SNS Assistant Director of Nutrition Services   South Washington County Schools rgrummon@sowashco.org  P: 651-425-6250    C: 651-336-1206   F: 651-425-6312 sowashco.org 7362 E Point Douglas Road South, Cottage Grove, MN 55016 

 COTTAGE GROVE  |  NEWPORT  |  ST. PAUL PARK  |  WOODBURY  |  AFTON  |  DENMARK  |  GREY CLOUD ISLAND

Hello Roberta,


There was and Enhancement with the recent update which did cause a change to where you click to view the menu. You may find more details on in the release notes (click Resources> Type Release > click on Release 16 link).  With this, the hyperlink to see and connect directly to the attached menu was shifted to the Grade Group in purple.  If you click there it should take you to the Menu just as before.   This enhancement was to provide 2 options for how to attach or select a menu in a cycle. 
 
We apologize for any confusion with this change.  Please let me know if you click where it says 6-8, Adult that all works for you like before or if you have any further questions.

turns.body_html:
Fruit + Veg reported as too low in nutrient analysis for June Summer Breakfast 25/26, week 1
Menu Cycle: June Summer Breakfast 25/26
Weekly cycle: 4 days
Threshold to pass: >= 4.00




Where as for in school year breakfast menu cycles, in December Breakfast Elementary 25/26, Week 1 they seem to be hitting 1-2 daily contributions towards Fruit + Veg:

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Melanie,     ​Upon further investigation I have found the issue.     In previous menus for in-school-year menu cycles, you had selected the choose minimum as 2 fruit, as we see in Breakfast Elem WK 1-1 Monday :9993 which is utilized in the december elementary breakfast cycle:            Which makes that days contribution as 1, as they can have both items:       However for the menus belonging to the June Summer Breakfast Cycle, they are restricted to 1 potential serving:            Which means their contribution would be limited to .5 contributions, and in order to achieve a contribution of <= 4.00 they would ideally need to be achieving 1 fruit contribution each day for all 4 days.     Thank you,           Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by

turns.body_html:
Hello Melanie,

​Upon further investigation I have found the issue.

In previous menus for in-school-year menu cycles, you had selected the choose minimum as 2 fruit, as we see in Breakfast Elem WK 1-1 Monday :9993 which is utilized in the december elementary breakfast cycle:


Which makes that days contribution as 1, as they can have both items:


However for the menus belonging to the June Summer Breakfast Cycle, they are restricted to 1 potential serving:


Which means their contribution would be limited to .5 contributions, and in order to achieve a contribution of <= 4.00 they would ideally need to be achieving 1 fruit contribution each day for all 4 days.

Thank you,

Hello Melanie,

​Upon further investigation I have found the issue.

In previous menus for in-school-year menu cycles, you had selected the choose minimum as 2 fruit, as we see in Breakfast Elem WK 1-1 Monday :9993 which is utilized in the december elementary breakfast cycle:


Which makes that days contribution as 1, as they can have both items:


However for the menus belonging to the June Summer Breakfast Cycle, they are restricted to 1 potential serving:


Which means their contribution would be limited to .5 contributions, and in order to achieve a contribution of <= 4.00 they would ideally need to be achieving 1 fruit contribution each day for all 4 days.

Thank you,

CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
 
 
 
 
 Dear Customer Support, 
   
 I’ve been working with the above referenced SFA, and his issue is typical of other SFAs we work with. 
   
 The SFA needs to discontinue numerous menu cycles they inherited from previous users going way back to 2014. The process of going through and individually deactivating each menu cycle is extremely time consuming. (see screenshot below) 
   
  
   
 It seems that if there was a box to check on the sides of these, that SFA’s could “clean up” their PrimeroEdge accounts much faster. We feel that this is why many schools hesitate to clean up their accounts. It’s just so time consuming. 
   
 Please review and advise. 
   
   
   
   



 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Richard Cummard 
 
 
PrimeroEdge Support Specialist 
 
 
CN Resource LLC

Mesa, AZ.  85213 
 
 
855.286.7685

ATTENTION: This email message is from an external sender. Do not open attachments or click links from unknown senders. To report suspicious email, use the Report Phishing button in Outlook.
*** SFA REMOVED ***
 
Hello PrimeroEdge Support,
 
I think that is a great idea. I would say prior to 2020. Some SFA’s do like to hang on to Menu Cycles that are up to five years old because they are reviewed every five years. However, we are seeing so many SFAs that have Menu Cycles in their accounts going back past ten years and it is just too time consuming for them to inactive one at a time.
 
Please advise if you want to proceed with this, or if PDE would like to weigh in on this matter.
 
 
 
Richard Cummard

Subject: Re: [External] PrimeroEdge PA - PENNSYLVANIA DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION - Discontinuing Menu Cycles and Menus-[#317524]
 
Hello CNR Helpdesk,
 
In PrimeroEdge, menu cycles are designed with data integrity and historical reporting in mind. There is probably more behind the scense going on with links to production etc.
There is not a way to currently discontinue menu cycles in bulk.
​
We could probably, on the back end, discontinue them in bulk PRIOR to a certain year.
​
Say before 2024 or before 2020.
​
​​

On Fri, Apr 17 at 11:18 AM , CNR Helpdesk <pahelp@cnresource.com> wrote:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Dear Customer Support,
 
I’ve been working with the above referenced SFA, and his issue is typical of other SFAs we work with.
 
The SFA needs to discontinue numerous menu cycles they inherited from previous users going way back to 2014. The process of going through and individually deactivating each menu cycle is extremely time consuming. (see screenshot below)
 
 
It seems that if there was a box to check on the sides of these, that SFA’s could “clean up” their PrimeroEdge accounts much faster. We feel that this is why many schools hesitate to clean up their accounts. It’s just so time consuming.
 
Please review and advise.
 
 
 
 
 
Richard Cummard 
PrimeroEdge Support Specialist 
CN Resource LLC  
1930 N. Arboleda, Suite 115 
Mesa, AZ.  85213 
855.286.7685 
Cnresource.com
