CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Dear Customer Support,

Yes, we would like to be to discontinue Menus as well.  I believe that will make your recent updates more effective.

It is felt that the best way to provide a list of Menus and Menu Cycles to PrimeroEdge for bulk deactivations would be able to provide legible screen shots of these. This way, the actual year the menu or menu cycle was created would not have to be in the name.

For example, if we were to provide you with a list of Menu Cycles from HOFFMAN HOMES to be discontinued, it would look like this. (see screenshot below)

[cid:image003.png@01DCD0BF.24848DB0]

Please advise as to how you would like to proceed.

Thank you.





[A blue and white letter n  AI-generated content may be incorrect.]
Richard Cummard

Mesa, AZ.  85213

Dear Customer Support,
 
Yes, we would like to be to discontinue Menus as well.  I believe that will make your recent updates more effective.
 
It is felt that the best way to provide a list of Menus and Menu Cycles to PrimeroEdge for bulk deactivations would be able to provide legible screen shots of these. This way, the actual year the menu or menu cycle was created would not have to be in the name.
 
For example, if we were to provide you with a list of Menu Cycles from HOFFMAN HOMES to be discontinued, it would look like this. (see screenshot below)
 
 
Please advise as to how you would like to proceed.
 
Thank you.

To: customercare@primeroedge.com; pahelp <pahelp@cnresource.com>
Cc: Ramp, Vonda <vramp@pa.gov>; Jarrett, Jennifer <jejarrett@pa.gov>; Southard, Lucien <c-lsouthar@pa.gov>
Subject: [External] PrimeroEdge PA - PENNSYLVANIA DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION - Discontinuing Menu Cycles and Menus-[#317524]
 
ATTENTION: This email message is from an external sender. Do not open attachments or click links from unknown senders. To report suspicious email, use the Report Phishing button in Outlook.
*** SFA REMOVED ***
 
Hello PrimeroEdge Support,
 
I think that is a great idea. I would say prior to 2020. Some SFA’s do like to hang on to Menu Cycles that are up to five years old because they are reviewed every five years. However, we are seeing so many SFAs that have Menu Cycles in their accounts going back past ten years and it is just too time consuming for them to inactive one at a time.
 
Please advise if you want to proceed with this, or if PDE would like to weigh in on this matter.
 
 
 
Richard Cummard 
PrimeroEdge Support Specialist 
CN Resource LLC  
1930 N. Arboleda, Suite 115 
Mesa, AZ.  85213 
855.286.7685 
Cnresource.com 
 
 
 
From: customercare@primeroedge.com <customercare@primeroedge.com>
Sent: Friday, April 17, 2026 12:34 PM
To: pahelp <pahelp@cnresource.com>
Cc: pahelp <pahelp@cnresource.com>; vramp@pa.gov; Jarrett, Jennifer <jejarrett@pa.gov>; c-lsouthar@pa.gov; ssease@hoffmanhomes.com
Subject: Re: [External] PrimeroEdge PA - PENNSYLVANIA DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION - Discontinuing Menu Cycles and Menus-[#317524]
 
Hello CNR Helpdesk,
 
In PrimeroEdge, menu cycles are designed with data integrity and historical reporting in mind. There is probably more behind the scense going on with links to production etc.
There is not a way to currently discontinue menu cycles in bulk.
​
We could probably, on the back end, discontinue them in bulk PRIOR to a certain year.
​
Say before 2024 or before 2020.
​
​​

On Fri, Apr 17 at 11:18 AM , CNR Helpdesk <pahelp@cnresource.com> wrote:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Dear Customer Support,
 
I’ve been working with the above referenced SFA, and his issue is typical of other SFAs we work with.
 
The SFA needs to discontinue numerous menu cycles they inherited from previous users going way back to 2014. The process of going through and individually deactivating each menu cycle is extremely time consuming. (see screenshot below)
 
 
It seems that if there was a box to check on the sides of these, that SFA’s could “clean up” their PrimeroEdge accounts much faster. We feel that this is why many schools hesitate to clean up their accounts. It’s just so time consuming.
 
Please review and advise.
 
 
 
 
 
Richard Cummard 
PrimeroEdge Support Specialist 
CN Resource LLC  
1930 N. Arboleda, Suite 115 
Mesa, AZ.  85213 
855.286.7685 
Cnresource.com

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Amy,   We reviewed the menu cycle and found that the planned serving counts were entered for Day 1 only. Days 2-5 have the menu-level planned meal count, but the item-level planned serving counts are blank.  The individual day view can still show the nutrient values for the menu items, but the weekly/combined nutrient analysis uses the saved item planned serving counts to calculate the weekly average. Since Days 2-5 do not have those counts entered, they are not included in the weekly nutrient total, which is why the weekly calories appear very low.  Please enter and save the planned serving counts for the menu items on Days 2-5, then rerun the weekly nutrient analysis.            Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket         Thank You      VIVEK ARRABELLI  Expert Care Specialist, Customer Care | PrimeroEdge  call: 866.442.6030    email: customercare@primeroedge.com  www.primeroedge.com      YOUR GOALS ARE OUR GOALS.
turns.body_html:
Hello Amy,

We reviewed the menu cycle and found that the planned serving counts were entered for Day 1 only. Days 2-5 have the menu-level planned meal count, but the item-level planned serving counts are blank.

The individual day view can still show the nutrient values for the menu items, but the weekly/combined nutrient analysis uses the saved item planned serving counts to calculate the weekly average. Since Days 2-5 do not have those counts entered, they are not included in the weekly nutrient total, which is why the weekly calories appear very low.

Please enter and save the planned serving counts for the menu items on Days 2-5, then rerun the weekly nutrient analysis.

Hello, Please see the details of the email below as reported by Jenny Edmondson. This was discovered by a field adviser during an Administrative Review.  SFA - South Allegheny  Thank you. ________________________________ From: Edmondson, Jennifer <jeedmondso@pa.gov> Sent: Thursday, May 14, 2026 2:01 PM To: Southard, Lucien <c-lsouthar@pa.gov> Subject: Menu Planning: (M) not showing in summary   Hi Lou,    Wanted to report a possible bug to PrimeroEdge. The attached reports were submitted to me yesterday by a field adviser for an upcoming review in TNG’s realm. The highlighted fruit punch NGR100392 on Day 1 shows (M) for all nutrients. The summary on the first page does not indicate that some of the nutrients are missing, however.    When I looked at the menu item itself, the nutrients were all there except for Added Sugars (snip below). Unfortunately, the SFA made changes to the menu cycle since the field adviser pulled the report,  including switching NGR100392 (discontinued) to SR107939.    Two things were odd: The report showed (M) for all nutrients in the fruit punch when it appeared that only Added Sugars was missing. Also, the summary did not pick up and display the (M)s at the top of page 1 to reflect the (M)s showing in the report for that item. SFA - South Allegheny

________________________________
From: Edmondson, Jennifer <jeedmondso@pa.gov>
Sent: Thursday, May 14, 2026 2:01 PM
To: Southard, Lucien <c-lsouthar@pa.gov>
Subject: Menu Planning: (M) not showing in summary


Hi Lou,



Wanted to report a possible bug to PrimeroEdge. The attached reports were submitted to me yesterday by a field adviser for an upcoming review in TNG’s realm. The highlighted fruit punch NGR100392 on Day 1 shows (M) for all nutrients. The summary on the first page does not indicate that some of the nutrients are missing, however.



When I looked at the menu item itself, the nutrients were all there except for Added Sugars (snip below). Unfortunately, the SFA made changes to the menu cycle since the field adviser pulled the report, including switching NGR100392 (discontinued) to SR107939.



Two things were odd: The report showed (M) for all nutrients in the fruit punch when it appeared that only Added Sugars was missing. Also, the summary did not pick up and display the (M)s at the top of page 1 to reflect the (M)s showing in the report for that item.



[cid:cc812482-f9f5-45ec-a652-63ca14f4d6a9]



Jenny

turns.body_text: The district has reported an issue where nutrient data is missing and is being denoted by (M) across the entire line for item NGR100392 on the Menu Cycle Week Nutrient Analysis Report.     In production, the issue has already been resolved by replacing NGR100392 with another fruit juice. However, the failing state still exists in UAT.     The ingredient report on the recipe page shows the nutrient information as expected. However, in the analysis of Menu Cycle SAHS Lunch Week 6 (25-26), the nutrient information is not populating in the nutrient report. The report displays (M) across the entire row.     I tested this on nutrition group UAT (Version 16.4.0), and the nutrition report returns (M) for the entire line in the nutrient analysis report. The recipe is currently discontinued. I clicked on recalculate nutrients for recipe NGR100392, but it did not change the nutrient analysis report. I then activated the recipe and clicked on recalculate, but nothing changed. I have since changed it back to discontinued state.     Another recipe NGR100327 also has Added Sugars, Vit D, Potassium, Moisture and Ash configured as missing, but on Menu Analysis, its showing all its nutrients to be missing.      Nutrition Group UAT (Version 16.4.0) > Menu Planning > Menus > Menu Cycle > SAHS Lunch Week 6 (25-26) > Analysis (nutrition report) > recipe NGR100392      Recipe NGR100392:

Hi Sai/ Michael,
​
Could you please look into this ticket?

The district has reported an issue where nutrient data is missing and is being denoted by (M) across the entire line for item NGR100392 on the Menu Cycle Week Nutrient Analysis Report.

In production, the issue has already been resolved by replacing NGR100392 with another fruit juice. However, the failing state still exists in UAT.

The ingredient report on the recipe page shows the nutrient information as expected. However, in the analysis of Menu Cycle SAHS Lunch Week 6 (25-26), the nutrient information is not populating in the nutrient report. The report displays (M) across the entire row.

We tested this on the nutrition group UAT (Version 16.4.0), and the nutrition report returns (M) for the entire line in the nutrient analysis report. The recipe is currently discontinued. We have since changed it back to discontinued status. 

Another recipe NGR100327 also has Added Sugars, Vit D, Potassium, Moisture and Ash configured as missing, but on Menu Analysis, its showing all its nutrients to be missing.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text: The analysis was missing the nutrient information for the recipe because the menu item is not convertible to serving size (the serving size in the recipe tab and menu item tab were not matching).    Once we updated the serving size in the recipe tab to 1 each and set the serving size to 1 each as default in the menu item tab and saved it, the nutrient information is showing in the analysis.   Please verify now and let us know if you require any further assistance.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text: Thank you for the follow-up question. You are correct that, at first glance, both the Recipe tab and the Menu Item tab appeared to be using the same serving size of “4 fluid ounces,” which is why the behavior was confusing.     What we found is that the issue was not with the displayed serving size itself, but rather with how the serving measurements were configured internally behind the scenes for nutrient analysis processing.     In the Recipe tab, although the serving size displayed as “4 fluid ounces,” the recipe was still configured with a Quantity of “1 Each.” Because of this, the system was internally treating the recipe serving as an “Each”-based serving rather than a fluid ounce–based serving during nutrient analysis calculations.     At the same time, the Menu Item tab was using a serving size configuration based on “4 fluid ounces.” During nutrient analysis, the application attempts to convert the recipe serving measurement to the menu serving measurement. Since the recipe was internally tied to “Each” while the menu item was tied to a fluid ounce serving measure, the system could not properly convert the values, which caused the nutrient analysis report to return “(M)” for missing nutrient information.     Once we updated both areas so they were aligned consistently as:   Recipe tab = 1 Each  Menu Item default serving size = 1 Each  the nutrient analysis processed successfully because the serving measurements matched internally and no conversion was required.     So in simpler terms, the issue was not the visible “4 fluid ounces” value itself, but rather that the recipe and menu item were using different underlying serving measurement types internally, even though the displayed serving sizes looked similar.     For reference, I have attached a screenshot highlighting the Recipe tab configuration that was causing the issue.   Please let me know if you have any additional questions.
turns.body_html:
Thank you for the follow-up question. You are correct that, at first glance, both the Recipe tab and the Menu Item tab appeared to be using the same serving size of “4 fluid ounces,” which is why the behavior was confusing.

What we found is that the issue was not with the displayed serving size itself, but rather with how the serving measurements were configured internally behind the scenes for nutrient analysis processing.

In the Recipe tab, although the serving size displayed as “4 fluid ounces,” the recipe was still configured with a Quantity of “1 Each.” Because of this, the system was internally treating the recipe serving as an “Each”-based serving rather than a fluid ounce–based serving during nutrient analysis calculations.

At the same time, the Menu Item tab was using a serving size configuration based on “4 fluid ounces.” During nutrient analysis, the application attempts to convert the recipe serving measurement to the menu serving measurement. Since the recipe was internally tied to “Each” while the menu item was tied to a fluid ounce serving measure, the system could not properly convert the values, which caused the nutrient analysis report to return “(M)” for missing nutrient information.

Once we updated both areas so they were aligned consistently as:
Recipe tab = 1 Each
Menu Item default serving size = 1 Each
the nutrient analysis processed successfully because the serving measurements matched internally and no conversion was required.

So in simpler terms, the issue was not the visible “4 fluid ounces” value itself, but rather that the recipe and menu item were using different underlying serving measurement types internally, even though the displayed serving sizes looked similar.

For reference, I have attached a screenshot highlighting the Recipe tab configuration that was causing the issue.
Please let me know if you have any additional questions.

In the Recipe tab, although the serving size displayed as “4 fluid ounces,” the recipe was still configured with a Quantity of “1 Each.” Because of this, the system was internally treating the recipe serving as an “Each”-based serving rather than a fluid ounce–based serving during nutrient analysis calculations.     At the same time, the Menu Item tab was using a serving size configuration based on “4 fluid ounces.” During nutrient analysis, the application attempts to convert the recipe serving measurement to the menu serving measurement. Since the recipe was internally tied to “Each” while the menu item was tied to a fluid ounce serving measure, the system could not properly convert the values, which caused the nutrient analysis report to return “(M)” for missing nutrient information.     Once we updated both areas so they were aligned consistently as:   Recipe tab = 1 Each  Menu Item default serving size = 1 Each  the nutrient analysis processed successfully because the serving measurements matched internally and no conversion was required.     So in simpler terms, the issue was not the visible “4 fluid ounces” value itself, but rather that the recipe and menu item were using different underlying serving measurement types internally, even though the displayed serving sizes looked similar. turns.body_html:
Hello Lou,


Thank you for the follow-up question. You are correct that, at first glance, both the Recipe tab and the Menu Item tab appeared to be using the same serving size of “4 fluid ounces,” which is why the behavior was confusing.

What we found is that the issue was not with the displayed serving size itself, but rather with how the serving measurements were configured internally behind the scenes for nutrient analysis processing.

In the Recipe tab, although the serving size displayed as “4 fluid ounces,” the recipe was still configured with a Quantity of “1 Each.” Because of this, the system was internally treating the recipe serving as an “Each”-based serving rather than a fluid ounce–based serving during nutrient analysis calculations.

At the same time, the Menu Item tab was using a serving size configuration based on “4 fluid ounces.” During nutrient analysis, the application attempts to convert the recipe serving measurement to the menu serving measurement. Since the recipe was internally tied to “Each” while the menu item was tied to a fluid ounce serving measure, the system could not properly convert the values, which caused the nutrient analysis report to return “(M)” for missing nutrient information.

Once we updated both areas so they were aligned consistently as:
Recipe tab = 1 Each
Menu Item default serving size = 1 Each
the nutrient analysis processed successfully because the serving measurements matched internally and no conversion was required.

So in simpler terms, the issue was not the visible “4 fluid ounces” value itself, but rather that the recipe and menu item were using different underlying serving measurement types internally, even though the displayed serving sizes looked similar. So, when we updated the value in recipe tab and menu item tab to 1 each, the analysis generated correctly. 

For reference, I have attached a screenshot highlighting the Recipe tab configuration that was causing the issue.
Please let me know if you have any additional questions.

ATTENTION: This email message is from an external sender. Do not open attachments or click links from unknown senders. To report suspicious email, use the Report Phish button in Outlook.
Hello Lou,


 
Thank you for the follow-up question. You are correct that, at first glance, both the Recipe tab and the Menu Item tab appeared to be using the same serving size of “4 fluid ounces,” which is why the behavior was confusing.
 
What we found is that the issue was not with the displayed serving size itself, but rather with how the serving measurements were configured internally behind the scenes for nutrient analysis processing.
 
In the Recipe tab, although the serving size displayed as “4 fluid ounces,” the recipe was still configured with a Quantity of “1 Each.” Because of this, the system was internally treating the recipe serving as an “Each”-based serving rather than a fluid ounce–based serving during nutrient analysis calculations.
 
At the same time, the Menu Item tab was using a serving size configuration based on “4 fluid ounces.” During nutrient analysis, the application attempts to convert the recipe serving measurement to the menu serving measurement. Since the recipe was internally tied to “Each” while the menu item was tied to a fluid ounce serving measure, the system could not properly convert the values, which caused the nutrient analysis report to return “(M)” for missing nutrient information.
 
Once we updated both areas so they were aligned consistently as:
Recipe tab = 1 Each
Menu Item default serving size = 1 Each
the nutrient analysis processed successfully because the serving measurements matched internally and no conversion was required.
 
So in simpler terms, the issue was not the visible “4 fluid ounces” value itself, but rather that the recipe and menu item were using different underlying serving measurement types internally, even though the displayed serving sizes looked similar. So, when we updated the value in recipe tab and menu item tab to 1 each, the analysis generated correctly. 
 
For reference, I have attached a screenshot highlighting the Recipe tab configuration that was causing the issue.
Please let me know if you have any additional questions.

The warning box you pasted talks about the “Serving Measures” on the recipe being convertible with “Serving Size” on the Menu. What I am seeing in TNG production right now for this menu item is that both tabs show 4 fluid ounces as the Serving Size. turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

We are working on a new dietitian and we need to clear out some of the old menu cycles or deactivate them, how do I do that?

[https://ci3.googleusercontent.com/mail-sig/AIorK4xdwjByxqQWrF_8FA6XAkwSk7dJg28WiWYZfElqweRqqiRr_zvA7to1eKP2gj-VZSpd9NHrEnKfg2uT]
turns.body_html:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
We are working on a new dietitian and we need to clear out some of the old menu cycles or deactivate them, how do I do that?

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Michelle,     You will need to go to:     Menu Planning-->Menus-->Menu Cycles     To deactivate a menu cycle, please filter the criteria to locate the specific cycle you wish to update. Next, click on the desired Menu Cycle (as shown in screenshot 1),   Uncheck the "Is Active" box, and click save(as shown in screenshot 2).   This will successfully deactivate the menu cycle in the system. Please let me know if you run into any issues or need further assistance!     1)       2)          Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket
turns.body_html:
Hello Michelle,

You will need to go to:

Menu Planning-->Menus-->Menu Cycles

To deactivate a menu cycle, please filter the criteria to locate the specific cycle you wish to update. Next, click on the desired Menu Cycle (as shown in screenshot 1), 
Uncheck the "Is Active" box, and click save(as shown in screenshot 2). 
This will successfully deactivate the menu cycle in the system. Please let me know if you run into any issues or need further assistance!
